decision: Agents run all bee commands themselves (bee_status, bee_cells, reservations, decisions, onboarding, verify); never hand a bee command to the user. Human actions = gate approvals, decision answers, privacy approvals only. Record: docs/decisions/0006.
rationale: Owner dogfood feedback: users kept being asked to run scripts by hand; the helpers are the agent interface, gates are the only human surface.

decision: bee-scribing capture is self-triggering: the agent detects settlement (explicit or silent) every turn and captures unprompted, announce-then-do, same turn. A user having to ask 'ghi lai' = detection failure. Record: docs/decisions/0007.
rationale: Owner dogfood feedback: capture only fired on manual request because the detection duty lived inside the skill, not in the always-loaded surfaces (AGENTS block, bee-hive).

decision: Adopt learn-harness-engineering items per docs/09: host-project standard commands live in .bee/config.json (commands.setup/start/test/verify — no init.sh, no new file); session baseline gate = run standard verify before claiming cells, broken baseline becomes a fix-first tiny cell; compounding promotion order = executable check first, critical-patterns prose fallback; friction layer taxonomy fixed at spec|context|environment|verification|state; denial-message contract = ERROR/WHY/FIX stated in 07-contracts.
rationale: Course diff (docs/09) showed the environment subsystem is bee's one real gap; user approved adopt-now items 1-5 on 2026-07-08. config.json over init.sh: the record is the primitive, scripts fork per-OS. Check-first promotion: prose decays, checks compound.

decision: Adopt docs/10: (A) fresh-session artifact generators — scribing bootstrap mode (skeleton system-overview + reading-map, provable facts only, user-approved run), command auto-detect with user confirmation, AGENTS.md outside-markers audit (propose-only via existing consent plan), preamble project-map lines, grooming probe items name the one-command fix; (B) PBI layer — docs/backlog.md product backlog (3 statuses proposed/in-flight/done, scribing-owned, specs pattern), proactive capture of deferred requests per 0007, chain wiring without new gates, optional pbi field on cells, repo artifact is source of truth over session todos. No hook enforcement of PBI transitions.
rationale: Fresh Session Test Q1-Q3 fail on legacy repos because bee detects holes but generates nothing; task layer is covered-and-stronger vs the PBI policy doc but the product-backlog layer above cells is genuinely missing — deferred requests die in chat. Settled with user 2026-07-08 (triển khai).

decision: Verify-string authoring discipline promoted to critical-patterns: dry-run metachar greps before dispatch; grep stable headings not invented tokens for prose cells.
rationale: harness10 shipped two verify-string defects that passed static review (invalid-regex grep -q '[' blocked a worker; grep-for-invented-token induced verify-gaming prose). Not cleanly mechanizable — a bracket lint false-positives on legitimate [ $(wc -l) ] bash tests — so prose promotion, meeting all three criteria.

decision: Capture stubs + background critique (decision 0017): same-turn durability via .bee/capture-queue.jsonl stub, full spec merge deferred to flush (wrap-up / PreCompact / next-session offer); high-risk lane still syncs inline; fresh-eyes and plan-checker run in background and block only their gate
rationale: Dogfood friction: mid-flow critique and full scribing sync stretched simple tasks and broke chat rhythm; splitting durability-now from elaboration-later keeps decisions 0003/0007/0011 guarantees at ~2s same-turn cost
alternatives: defer everything to feature close (pre-0003, capture dies); skip critique in simple lanes (loses the pass); opt-in flow-mode toggle (slow path would stay default)

decision: Orchestrator goal-check + frozen judge (decision 0018, P12): a [DONE] counts only after the orchestrator re-runs the cell verify itself (lane-scaled) and bee_cells judge shows no undeclared test/CI/lockfile/verify-config changes; hits flag the cell for review, task misses re-dispatch same tier
rationale: LOOP survey: evidence-not-assertion must live at the orchestrator; a worker that rewrites the judge is not passing it; prerequisite for external workers

decision: External executor tiers (decision 0019, P14): a configurable tier may be {kind:cli, command} — resolveTier returns inherit/model/budget/cli; external workers run the same bee-executing contract via prompt-file dispatch + job-log tending; always goal-checked, secrets stay provider-local
rationale: Widens the decision-0012 cost lever to any provider CLI (GPT/GLM/Kimi) without forking the worker contract; mechanics from codex-orchestrator-graph and delegator

decision: Unknowns toolkit (decision 0020, P9-P11): exploring teaches before asking when the user is guessing; walkthrough offers an optional 3-5 question quiz; SEE gray areas may lock via a throwaway .spikes mock — the one exception to exploring's no-code rule
rationale: Thariq map-vs-territory: a decision locked from a guessed answer is fake; the quiz mechanizes the gate litmus; visual decisions need reaction surfaces, not descriptions

decision: Review slot + effort knob (decision 0021, P16/P17): models map defaults to the all-Claude role split — session model orchestrates, opus reviews (reviewing specialists, fresh-eyes, plan-checker), sonnet implements, haiku extracts — every slot editable per repo (string | null | {model,effort} | {kind:cli}); null review falls back to generation
rationale: Owner adopted the Fable/Opus/Sonnet/GPT role split as default: the model that reviews should not be the model that implemented; editability keeps it honest for users with only one subscription

decision: External-dispatch field refinements (from the codex-first pattern): result via -o file (never parse JSONL), stderr suppressed by default, rescue prefers codex exec resume --last with the failing verify output (max 2 rounds then BLOCKED), session-tool/MCP/secrets/destructive cells never route to cli executors, workspace-write only — no --yolo default
rationale: A practitioner skill running the same Claude-specs/Codex-implements split surfaced the operational edges bee's 0019 protocol lacked: cheap context-preserving retries, output hygiene, and tool-dependency dispatch guards

decision: Evolving loop design settled (P18): dogfood repos stay zero-effort (digest is a compounding side effect); bee-evolving runs only in the bee repo, on demand; improvements go through the Iron Law (failing pressure test first); two human gates (what to fix, approve push) — push never auto
rationale: Owner wants bee to learn from real anphabe-gogl usage and ship improvements to GitHub; separation keeps project decisions and bee-design decisions in different sessions, and gated push keeps self-modification under the same discipline bee imposes on everything else

decision: Iron Law binds skill edits with no mechanical-edit exemption: a cell that edits any SKILL.md carries RED/GREEN pressure-test evidence, even when it only adds a numbered step invoking an already-verified command
rationale: Owner chose (a) at Gate 2 of the evolving loop (P18). Carving an exemption into the one law bee uses to protect itself from its own edits would be the first crack; bee-writing-skills already predicts the rationalization ('it is just one step'). Cost is bounded — a pressure scenario per skill edit — and the loop being built here is precisely the machinery that will edit skills at scale
alternatives: Mechanical-edit exemption for step-only additions that introduce no rule, discipline, or refusal (rejected by owner)

decision: Evolving loop design (P18, v2): (D1) dogfood repos stay zero-effort — digest is a compounding side effect; (D2 REVISED) the digest is an ALLOWLIST of structured fields — kind, layer, source, title, first_seen, pain — and carries NO free detail/text field, because measurement showed friction prose routinely names functions, files and config keys that no code-block strip or secret regex removes; (D2b) the CONSUMER revalidates and datamarks every foreign digest entry before it can enter a prompt, and every source path is realpath-contained; (D3) bee-evolving runs only in the bee repo, on demand; (D4) improvements go through the Iron Law (failing pressure test first, edits included); (D5) two human gates (what to fix, approve push) — push never auto
rationale: Validating slice A measured the real corpus across 5 bee repos and found the original D2 unenforceable: friction detail text is unfenced prose containing identifiers (readBacklogCounts, COMMAND_KEYS, approved_gates.shape, internal call graphs), which passes code-block stripping, passes the secret and injection regexes, and fits inside the 600-char cap. The plan had already shrunk D2 in prose without superseding it, violating its own prohibition. Owner chose the allowlist over a weakened promise: drop free text entirely rather than advertise a guarantee the pipeline cannot keep. D2b is added because the security panel showed the redaction boundary sat at the producer while bee-evolving — the consumer — is the party that edits its own source from foreign files; write-time scanning protects the wrong party, and a symlink under a foreign .bee/ defeats a string-prefix scope check

decision: Realpath containment is the only read-scope guard
rationale: Every filesystem read routes through resolveInScope(root, relPath) which path.resolve-normalizes then fs.realpath-validates against the repo root. fs.realpath rejects symlink escapes; a symlinked .bee/cells/evil.json pointing outside the repo is warned and never read. This is a read-scope invariant, not a sandbox. Bypassing it with fs.promises or named imports creates a silent escape hatch and must be prevented by source-level drift testing.

decision: Digest is an allowlist snapshot with no free-text fields
rationale: Each digest entry carries exactly six fields: kind, layer, source, title, first_seen, pain. No detail/text/outcome/deviations field. This removes surface cross-file dependencies from reachable data. A friction entry naming readBacklogCounts and COMMAND_KEYS leaves neither string in digest bytes. ENTRY_FIELDS, KIND_ALIASES, and DROP_REASONS are single-sourced and drift-tested to prevent accidental divergence. Import these constants rather than hardcoding copies in multiple modules.

decision: Foreign digest consumer revalidation: re-scan, datamark, allowlist enforcement
rationale: The producing repo's write-time scan does not protect the consumer. A hand-edited or hostile digest is just JSON on disk. For every foreign entry: resolve the path and reject symlink escapes by realpath; re-run SECRET_CONTENT_PATTERNS and INJECTION_PATTERNS dropping violators; wrap surviving titles in datamark() before they can enter a prompt (this prevents injection attacks); enforce ENTRY_FIELDS allowlist, never merging extra fields through. A missing or corrupt digest is warned and skipped, never thrown.

decision: A frozen assertion that encodes a defect is unfrozen by the planner/orchestrator, never by the worker: test_lib.mjs:1833 asserted a foreign entry's source survives mergeDigests raw ('src'), which IS the P1-1 vulnerability. Updating it to require datamarking is a strengthening, not a weakening, and the worker correctly returned [BLOCKED] rather than rewrite it
rationale: Cell evolving-3 wrote both the consumer-side boundary and the test that accepted its own gap; 93 green assertions therefore proved conformance to a wrong spec, not safety. Two opus reviewers reading the code missed that the suite blessed the defect — it surfaced only when a worker tried to fix the bug and the suite refused. The frozen-assertion rule must keep its escape hatch (worker STOPS and quotes the assertion) or the first defect encoded in a test becomes permanent
alternatives: Let the worker silently update the assertion (rejected: indistinguishable from weakening a test to make a change pass). Keep the assertion and drop the fix (rejected: ships the P1)

decision: Second narrow unfreeze (evolving-7): the ENTRY_FIELDS source-literal grep in test_lib.mjs:1421-1422 is deleted; the value-lock, the no-free-text check, and the SCHEMA_VERSION/DROP_REASONS literal pins all stay. A drift guard that regex-matches a module's own source pins SYNTAX, not behavior — and here the pinned syntax (a bare name array) was the defect itself, blocking the structural fix that makes forgetting a field impossible
rationale: Third occurrence of one defect class: mergeDigests neutralized only the fields someone remembered. The fix replaces ENTRY_FIELDS with ENTRY_FIELD_SPEC (field -> validator) and derives the name list from it, so an unspecced field cannot exist. The frozen grep demanded the literal array and thus forbade the fix. The repo already logged this exact anti-pattern against BACKLOG_STATUSES ('a value-lock, not a cross-file guard — regex-matching its own source guards nothing'), so this is a known, twice-seen failure mode, and the second worker to hit a defect-encoding assertion again correctly returned [BLOCKED] instead of rewriting it
alternatives: Keep the grep and patch only first_seen (rejected: field-by-field patching is what failed in rounds 1 and 2). Let the worker delete the assertion itself (rejected: indistinguishable from weakening a test to pass)

decision: Prefer an allowlist of structured fields over a blocklist filter whenever the data is human-authored prose: prose reliably embeds identifiers, and no strip or regex finds them. And place a redaction boundary at the party AT RISK, not the party producing the data — in a self-modifying or cross-trust pipeline, write-time validation protects the wrong party
rationale: Two independent surprises in P18 that neither the plan, the advisor, nor the first validation pass predicted. (1) Measurement showed the code-block strip removed exactly zero characters from real friction prose, which named functions, files, and config keys in ordinary sentences; the honest fix was deleting the field, not sharpening the filter. (2) The producing repo's write-time scan guards that repo against its own workers. bee-evolving reads foreign digests to edit bee's own source, so bee is the party at risk and must revalidate at read time — found only by a security lens explicitly asked who is at risk
alternatives: Sharpen the blocklist (rejected: measured no-op). Single write-time boundary (rejected: protects the producer, not the consumer)

decision: Gate 4 evolving-loop slice B: owner explicitly acknowledged merging with 2 open P1s (rank --json emits stripped key; normalizeTitle duplicates datamark cleaning) — both filed to backlog as fix-first candidates; review record review-slice-b.md
rationale: Owner's explicit Gate 4 choice on 2026-07-10; both P1s are corroboration-promoted defense-in-depth gaps (code boundary intact per security review), concrete fixes recorded in backlog so nothing evaporates

decision: Build self-modifying skills at the strongest tier, but pressure-probe their gates with the weakest plausible model: evolving-10 was authored at ceiling while all four RED/GREEN scenarios ran against fresh haiku subagents — a refusal only the strong model produces is not a guardrail
rationale: All four RED failures were plausible middle options invented by the weak model (patch-on-branch, standing pre-approval, runbook push); the GREEN pass only held because the skill forecloses each rationalization by name. Probe strength and build strength are independent dispatch decisions

decision: Lane scaling v2: ceremony now truly scales — docs lane (knowledge-only files: no gates/cells/reviewers, announce-then-do + capture); tiny/small merge Gates 2+3 into one shape+execution question with the reality check inline in planning (bee-validating not separately invoked, 0 validating subagents); tiny/small execute solo in-session (no swarm workers, cell discipline kept); review scales: tiny = self-review + done-report (no Gate 4 question), small = 1 correctness reviewer, standard = 4 core + learnings pair, high-risk = full wave + conditionals
rationale: config-sample (a tiny sample-file task) burned ~9 subagent dispatches and 4 human stops because validating/swarming/reviewing had no lane conditions despite the 'lanes scale ceremony, never memory' principle. Owner confirmed all 4 design choices via structured Q&A: self-review for tiny, solo execution for tiny/small, 1 merged gate for tiny, dedicated docs lane. Knowledge machinery (decision log, capture stubs, spec sync) intentionally unchanged in every lane — it was never the bottleneck

decision: config-sample.json lives at .bee/config-sample.json (owner instruction 2026-07-10), superseding the config-sample feature's root-location choice (D1) and cell config-sample-2's 'no .bee/config-sample.json' truth
rationale: Owner wants the sample colocated with bee's runtime config directory. Content unchanged; no living doc referenced the root path

decision: External-executor finish contract (external-result-contract): a cli worker's last FILE act is writing .bee/workers/<cell-id>.result.json — outcome is exactly the four status tokens (the cli transport of the one worker contract) — and the orchestrator accepts by file, never by exit: missing/unparseable/invalid result = failed run into the rescue ladder. The prompt file at .bee/workers/<cell-id>.prompt.md IS the durable contract; resume rounds reference it and never re-pass dispatch-time flags (resume inherits sandbox/config). Rejected from the repository-harness source: partial/needs_intake outcomes, schema-validator tooling, preemptive worktree-shim machinery (one conditional sentence only).
rationale: Adapted from repository-harness SYMPHONY_SCOPE 4.4/4.6 after the codex-first refinements (29b7f7bb) left silent-death detection undefined. Field-proven same day: the feature's own review ran the protocol (codex reviewer, -o result, 2 resume rescues); an independent cross-model reviewer caught 5 real findings including two author self-contradictions. Acceptance rules ported without their machinery keep the harness lean.

decision: Gate 4 learnings-pair-relocation: owner chose fix-then-close; both P1s (wave cap refill, 0021 tier drift) and the P3 fixed in cell -2, grep-verified, feature approved for close
rationale: Owner decision 2026-07-11 via structured gate question; fixes are one-phrase gated_auto items verified by the cell verify chain (negative+positive greps, test_lib 124/0)

decision: Review wave composition (learnings-pair-relocation): standard lane = exactly 4 core reviewers, wave cap 6 (4 core + max 2 conditionals on the review slot); synthesis (dedupe, corroboration promotion, autofix_class, severity counts) is the orchestrator's OWN inline duty after ALL reviewers return — never a dispatched subagent; precedent flows planning bootstrap -> plan.md -> reviewers, no review-time learnings dispatch. Supersedes decision 0021's researcher=extraction/synthesizer=ceiling tier clause (those agents no longer exist); knowledge machinery unchanged in every lane.
rationale: Owner-approved 2026-07-11: review-time precedent search only annotated findings after code existed (planning already owned the search per its bootstrap); the synthesizer subagent ran on the orchestrator's model so it added a dispatch, not a mind. -2 dispatches per standard/high-risk feature. Cap 7->6 was a reviewer-forced correction: a capacity constant derived from the old roster would have silently refilled the freed slots.
alternatives: Literal relocation (add researcher step to planning, synthesis step to compounding) rejected as duplication - both already owned the duty. Keeping the pair rejected: 2 dispatches per feature for annotation-after-the-fact.

decision: Gate 4 skill-sync: owner approved merge after the fix wave closed all 9 code-confirmed P1s (02662bf hardening, 3d36b22 contract); UAT RUN item passed live (apply synced drifted bee-hive, recheck up_to_date with hash parity); 7 P2 hardening items filed to backlog non-blocking
rationale: Owner decision 2026-07-11 via structured gate question; every fix red-first with recorded pre-fix failures, orchestrator goal-checked both cells in the session shell

decision: skill-sync shipped policies (supplement D1-D5, all Gate-3/4-covered): production target is hard-coded os.homedir()/.claude/skills with NO override (tests isolate via spawned-process fake HOME/USERPROFILE); symlinked bee-* entries or inner symlinks = blocked_symlink, skipped loudly, never written/unlinked/deleted; absent tree = fresh install vs existing-unreadable = unknown = refuse; --force-downgrade only when all three versions resolve numeric AND the dry-run exposed the items it will mutate (forced_downgrade:true reported); skill plan items carry scope: installed|source; case-alias collisions = blocked_alias fail-closed; repoRoot-targetRoot overlap refuses pre-write
rationale: Owner-approved through Gates 3/4 on 2026-07-11; each policy is enforced by red-first tests (232+ checks incl. a real case-insensitive /mnt/c run) after the review wave found 9 code-confirmed P1s

decision: Gate 3 model-tier-guard: auto-approved via gate bypass (standard lane, no hard-gate flag) — verdict READY WITH CONSTRAINTS after reality gate PASS, 7/7 feasibility rows PROVEN, 3 plan-check iterations + orchestrator-verified mechanical closure of the last cell-3 grep finding; constraints: cell-4 transaction blast radius noted, live-fire deny is orchestrator-owned at acceptance + Gate 4 UAT with reopen rule
rationale: Owner enabled bee-bypass-gate; advisor consult at execution point satisfied inline (session model = configured advisor model fable); full report docs/history/model-tier-guard/reports/validation-slice1.md

decision: 0023 — explicit-tier transport: every Agent/Task dispatch now carries an explicit tier (model param or [bee-tier: <tier>] marker); bare dispatches are denied by the new bee-model-guard PreToolUse hook. Amends only the transport clause of decision 0015 (omit-param alone no longer means ceiling — omit-param + marker does); the principle that the ceiling is the session model, never configured, stands unchanged. Written as docs/decisions/0023-explicit-tier-transport.md with a single Status amendment line added to 0015.
rationale: Owner observed during a live session that tier_mix.ceilingShare=0.4 with 21 untiered cells — dispatches were silently inheriting the expensive session model because omitting the model param was ambiguous between deliberate ceiling and a forgotten tier. The model-tier-guard feature (cells 1-3, capped) built the hook, registration, and skill-doc wording; this cell records the decision and applies onboarding to vendor/wire it.

decision: Gate 4 model-tier-guard: owner approved merge after the fix wave closed all 5 corroborated P1 clusters (c943def anchor+fail-open+Task, d98a730 structural assertions, 1419a45 docs+re-vendor); UAT live-fire battery confirmed by owner (bare DENY, embedded-marker DENY, anchored-marker PASS, model-param PASS); 2 P3 filed to backlog non-blocking
rationale: Owner decision 2026-07-11 via structured gate question; every fix red-first with recorded pre-fix failures; orchestrator goal-checked all 7 cells and ran the live-fire battery in the session itself

decision: Validation iteration cap triage (model-tier-guard precedent): at the 3-iteration plan-check cap, a residual finding that is deterministic and mechanical — the reviewer prescribed the exact fix and supplied counterexamples — may be closed by the orchestrator applying that fix verbatim and proving it with command evidence against the reviewer's own probes; only findings needing design judgment escalate to the user. Never a 4th review iteration either way.
rationale: model-tier-guard finding 6 (case-insensitive greps) closed this way 2026-07-11: both reviewer counterexamples rejected, true text passes, recorded in validation-slice1.md; saves a full external review cycle for a fixed-string swap

decision: Gate 4 grill-deltas: merge auto-approved under gate-bypass — P1=0 after two review-fix cells (grill-deltas-3/4 closed both temporal-contradiction P1s from the external codex reviewer; round-3 re-review NO FINDINGS / PASS), zero SEE/CALL/RUN UAT items (no CONTEXT.md, prose-only skill feature), suites green (test_lib 124/0, onboard PASS)
rationale: decision 0010 bypass conditions met: no open P1, no UAT item to fail; audit line posted in chat; report docs/history/grill-deltas/reports/review.md

decision: External skill/library adoption follows reuse-over-install: map the upstream against local equivalents first; fold only genuine deltas into existing skills as prose branches; never install a parallel gate-less pipeline competitor (grill-for-unknowns precedent — ~90% was already decision 0020; only the materiality test and Terms glossary were real deltas, shipped as grill-deltas)
rationale: an ungated single-skill import of exploring→briefing territory would compete with Gates 1-3; the anti-reinvention ladder applies to skills exactly as to code; anchor evidence docs/history/research/grill-for-unknowns.md

decision: Dispatch audit log contract (P22): .bee/logs/dispatch.jsonl is fail-open best-effort — one line per evaluated Agent/Task dispatch {ts,tool,transport,model,tier,subagent_type,description<=120}; a log failure never changes the guard's decision/exit code; nothing downstream may assume 100% capture; deny/allow semantics of decision 0023 explicitly untouched
rationale: auditing must never block a dispatch (same fail-open shape as logDeny/logCrash); owner's need is visibility, not enforcement — enforcement stays with the 0023 transport rules
